rulings.Case Title: THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. ALAN ARGOSINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Release Bond Of $2500 To Cory Narog. Narog Is The Prevailing Party
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CORY NAROG's Motion To Release Bond Of $2500 To Cory Narog. Narog Is The Prevailing Party Cross-complainant Cory Narog's motion for release of $2500 bond is granted. Per the court's records, Mr. Narog is entitled to release of the bond he posted on April 11, 2012. The court will sign the proposed order submitted by Mr. Narog after making a few modifications to it. rulings.Case Title: DROMHUS, INC. VS. ANNA GATTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Perry E. Rhoads In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER DROMHUS, INC.'s Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Perry E. Rhoads In Support Thereof Petitioner Dromhus, Inc.'s petition to confirm arbitration award is off calendar. Dromhus fails to show that it properly served the petition in compliance with CCP 1290.4. Paragraph 12 of the parties' settlement agreement refers to service of a notice of default. It does not govern service of this petition to confirm arbitration award. Dromhus also fails to demonstrate proper substitute service. The declaration of Anna Gatti states that Ms. Gatti resides in Florence, Italy and never resided at the 690 Market Street address. Dromhus failed to effectuate proper service in accordance with CCP 1290.4 or 415.20, and/or the Hague Convention. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION Petitioner International Union of Operating Engineers Stationary Engineers, Local 39's petition to compel arbitration is denied. The Union bears the burden of showing that Jason Fong's dispute regarding his release from employment is arbitrable and it failed to do so. Paragraph 44 of the MOU provides that "permanent employees or employees who have satisfactorily completed the probationary period may" arbitrate their release from employment. Even if the court considered the Apprentice Agreement submitted with the reply papers, the Union has not provided any evidence that Mr. Fong was either a permanent employee or that he satisfactorily completed a probationary period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: NG SOLUTIONS, LLC, VS. OAKVILLE CAPITAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC's Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees Plaintiff NG Solutions, LLC's motion for an award of attorney's fees is granted. NG is awarded $52,840 in fees against defendants Oakville Capital, Inc. and Aaron Wheeler. NG has shown it is entitled to an award of fees as the prevailing party in this case per the fees provision in the parties' agreement. The hourly rates and asserted time spent of NG's counsel is reasonable and comports with the activities undertaken by NG's counsel. The time spent by NG's counsel is adequately supported by the composite statement of account attached as exhibit B to Mr. Strom's declaration. While defendants contend that most or some of the services performed by NG's counsel was unnecessary, defendants have not identified any specific litigation activity that was either unnecessary, unreasonable or took longer than it should have taken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for NG is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Murali Ranjithan, M.D.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 7, (1 of 2) DEFENDANT MURALI RANJITHAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT M.D.S, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues Defendant Murali Ranjithan's motion for summary judgment is granted based on the one year statute of limitations of CCP 340.5. Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co., (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1110-1 states: "Under the discovery rule, the statute of limitations begins to run when the plaintiff suspects or should suspect that her injury was caused by wrongdoing, that someone has done something wrong to her¿[T]he limitations period begins once the plaintiff has notice or information of circumstances to put a reasonable person on inquiry....A plaintiff need not be aware of the specific facts necessary to establish the claim; that is a process contemplated by pretrial discovery. Once the plaintiff has a suspicion of wrongdoing, and therefore an incentive to sue, she must decide whether to file suit or sit on her rights. So long as a suspicion exists, it is clear that the plaintiff must go find the facts; she cannot wait for the facts to find her. (citations and internal quotation marks omitted)). "It is a plaintiff's suspicion of negligence, rather than an expert's opinion, that triggers the limitation period. The limitations period begins when the plaintiff's suspicions are aroused." (Knowles v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1300 (emphasis in original).) The undisputed facts shown that plaintiffs Gary and Beverly Presley-Nelson were on inquiry notice by the latest October 19, 2015 and filed this untimely lawsuit on March 15, 2017. The following facts establish that plaintiffs' claims are time-barred as a matter of law: 1) Mr. Presley-Nelson previously had a brain tumor in 1980 (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 426:1-427:6); 2) Mr. Presley-Nelson knew that one of the purposes of the MRIs was to determine whether his tumor recurred (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 435:22-436:2); 3) On October 14, 2008 Dr. King-Stephens told Mr. Presley-Nelson that the tumor was gone and he would never need an MRI again (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo. Vol. 1, 153:12-154:2; 157:6-12); 4) Mr. Presley-Nelson told his wife that the results of the 2008 MRI were fine (Beverly Presley-Nelson Depo., 86:3-7); 5) Mr. Presley-Nelson suffered a significant seizure in April of 2015 that was more impactful than prior seizures (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 439:8-23; 441:9-442:1); 6) After April 28, 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson did not return to Dr. King-Stephens because Mr. Presley-Nelson and his wife suspected that Dr. King-Stephens "was not going down the right path" because he would not order another MRI (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 1, 210:18-211:10; 228:16-229:20); 7) Dr. Cornes performed an MRI and plaintiff learned that he had a brain tumor on or around July 27, 2015 (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 443:21-444:25; Vol. 1, 245:5-246:2); 8) Mr. Presley-Nelson suspected that his seizures and memory problems were attributable to the tumor (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 446:25-447:6; 447:12-448:11); 9) In August of 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson inquired regarding how the tumor could come back (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 449:7-450:15); and 10) On October 19, 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson requested his medical records from Dr. King-Stephens because Mr. Presley-Nelson questioned how the tumor could recur when Dr. King-Stephens told him that it would not (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 513:15-514:13). =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Murali Ranjithan Motion For Summary Judgment M.D.S, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 7, (2 of 2) Defendant Murali Ranjithan Motion For Summary Judgment M.D.S, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues By October 19, 2015 at the latest, plaintiffs were aware of the return of the tumor and Dr. King-Stephens may have wronged them. Plaintiffs' ignorance regarding Dr. Ranjithan's identity or his precise role in the alleged wrongdoing does not toll the statute of limitations. The statute of limitations begins to run when the plaintiff suspects or should suspect that his injury was caused by wrongdoing and "[a]ggrieved parties generally need not know the exact manner in which their injuries were 'effected, nor the identities of all parties who may have played a role therein.'" (Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Industries (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 932.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Dr. Ranjithan is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling) =302/hk

Pico; Supporting Evidence Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.'s motion for summary judgment is granted based on the one year statute of limitations of CCP 340.5. Jolly v. "It is a plaintiff's suspicion of negligence, rather than an expert's opinion, that triggers the limitation period. The limitations period begins when the plaintiff's suspicions are aroused." (Knowles v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1300 (emphasis in original).) Vol. 1, 153:12-154:2; 157:6-12); 4) Mr. Presley-Nelson told his wife that the results of the 2008 MRI were fine (Beverly Presley-Nelson Depo., 86:3-7); 5) Mr. Presley-Nelson suffered a significant seizure in April of 2015 that was more impactful than prior seizures (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 439:8-23; 441:9-442:1); 6) After April 28, 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson did not return to Dr. King-Stephens because Mr. Presley-Nelson and his wife suspected that Dr. King-Stephens "was not going down the right path" because he would not order another MRI (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 1, 210:18-211:10; 228:16-229:20); 7) Dr. Cornes performed an MRI and plaintiff learned that he had a brain tumor on or around July 27, 2015 (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 443:21-444:25; Vol. 1, 245:5-246:2); 8) Mr. Presley-Nelson suspected that his seizures and memory problems were attributable to the tumor (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 446:25-447:6; 447:12-448:11); 9) In August of 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson inquired regarding how the tumor could come back (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 449:7-450:15); and 10) On October 19, 2015 Mr. Presley-Nelson requested his medical records from Dr. King-Stephens because Mr. Presley-Nelson questioned how the tumor could recur when Dr. King-Stephens told him that it would not (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 513:15-514:13). =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant David King-Stephens Motion For Summary Judgment M.D.S Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 7, (2 of 2) Defendant David King-Stephens Motion For Summary Judgment M.D.S Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence (For Tentative Ruling Only) By October 19, 2015 at the latest, plaintiffs were aware of the return of the tumor and Dr. King-Stephens may have wronged them. The service of the notice to commence action per CCP 364 on October 31, 2016 did not extend the statute of limitations. By that time, the limitations period had already run. Plaintiffs fail to present evidence showing that Dr. King-Stephens is estopped from raising the statute of limitations. While Dr. King-Stephens assured plaintiffs that an additional MRI was unnecessary, plaintiffs knew that that advice was erroneous no later than October 19, 2015 and Dr. King-Stephens did nothing at that time to induce plaintiffs to sit on their rights. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance to depose defendant's expert, Dr. Fisher is denied. A continuance is unnecessary because this case is time- barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Dr. King-Stephens is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling) =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: NP APARTMENTS, LLC. VS. ASSOCIATED MATERIALS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Exclude The Opinions Of Defendant Associated Materials Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NP APARTMENTS, LLC's Motion To Exclude The Opinions Of Defendant Associated Materials Expert Witnesses Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear; argument required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. ALBERTO MENDOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons [Ccp 418.10]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT ALBERTO MENDOZA's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons [Ccp 418.10] Defendant Albert Mendoza's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff Wells Fargo has not shown that it validly served Mr. Mendoza by substitute service. The court strongly encourages Mr. Mendoza to make a general appearance in this case notwithstanding that he has not yet been validly served. Absent a voluntary general appearance by Mr. Mendoza, the court suggests that Wells Fargo use the notice and acknowledgment procedure and, if Mr. Mendoza fails to acknowledge service, he will be liable for what could be very substantial costs of service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Mendoza is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JOHN REED VS. TRANSPORTATION BROKERAGE SPECIALISTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Trevor R. Witt, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT TRANSPORTATION BROKERAGE SPECIALISTS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Trevor R. Witt, Esq. Defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialists, Inc's motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending the completion of the arbitration of plaintiff John Reed's individual, victim-specific claims. Mr. Reed has not shown that Transportation Brokerage waived its right to seek arbitration of Mr. Reed's victim-specific claims. Transportation Brokerage's delay in seeking to compel arbitration was not unreasonable in light of the circumstances of this case nor did Transportation Brokerage engage in litigation conduct inconsistent with its right to seek enforcement of the parties 'arbitration agreement. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195 ("a party who resists arbitration on the ground of waiver bears a heavy burden, and any doubts regarding a waiver allegation should be resolved in favor of arbitration.")). Transportation Brokerage consistently informed Mr. Reed of its intent to seek arbitration of Mr. Reed's victim-specific claims and the pendency of efforts to mediate the dispute is a reasonable explanation for the delay. Mr. Reed has not shown that Transportation Brokerage took advantage of judicial discovery procedures not available in arbitration because Transportation Brokerage did not propound formal discovery, advised Mr. Reed that before any formal discovery starts the parties need to resolve the issue of arbitration of victim-specific claims, and answered a set of discovery propounded by Mr. Reed with objections for the purpose of preserving them. Transportation Brokerage's only pleading was an answer to the complaint in which it stated as an affirmative defense the arbitration agreement, and it did not file any other pleadings or motions and did not propound any discovery unlike defendants in the cases relied on by Mr. Reed. Per Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1228, which is currently binding on-point authority, Mr. Reed's claims for recovery of unpaid wages seek individual victim-specific relief that is covered by the enforceable arbitration language in the parties' agreement. In the interest of judicial economy and to avoid possibly inconsistent results, Mr. Reed's representative claims for PAGA civil penalties are stayed pending the resolution of the arbitration proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Transportation Brokerage is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MIRNA VASQUEZ VS. HERTZ VEHICLES ,LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Beverly Harrison And Richard Harrison And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,740.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Sandeep G. Agarwal, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MIRNA VASQUEZ' Motion To Compel Depositions Of Beverly Harrison And Richard Harrison And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,740.00 Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED without prejudice. There is no basis for the Plaintiff's request that the subject depositions take place in California. Plaintiff's counsel has set remote depositions in this case and these depositions are no different. Defendant's counsel has presented reasonable explanations for the current scheduling delays and offered multiple alternative dates for the depositions to take place. If the Defendants do not appear on the date(s) offered, Plaintiff may renew this motion. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL MOORE VS. PILOT AI LABS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Trinnet Group, Inc.S And Trinet Hr Iii-A, Inc.S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTs PILOT AI LABS, INC., JONATHAN SU, and ROBERT ENGLISH's Joinder In Trinnet Group, Inc.S And Trinet Hr Iii-A, Inc.S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Defendants Pilot AI Labs, Inc., Jonathan Su, and Robert English's joinder in Defendants Trinet Group, Inc. and Trinet HR III-A, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied. The Dispute Resolution Protocol in TriNet's terms and conditions requires Pilot AI to seek to compel arbitration under its own employment agreement with Plaintiff Rachel Moore. The DRP indicates that separate agreements between its customers and its customer's employees (such as the employment agreement between Pilot AI and Ms. Moore) control disputes to the extent such agreements are inconsistent with the DRP. The employment agreement's arbitration clause contains materially different terms. Arbitration is specifically sited in Santa Clara County and the arbitration is governed by the California Arbitration Act rather than the Federal Arbitration Act. Moreover, the employment agreement's integration clause suggests that other agreements like the DRP should not apply or conflict with the exclusive agreement between Pilot AI and Ms. Moore. Therefore, the employment agreement controls Ms. Moore's claims against Pilot AI, Mr. Su, and Mr. English, not the DRP. The Pilot AI defendants are only third-party beneficiaries of the DRP to the extent their own arbitration clause with Ms. Moore does not conflict with the DRP. Similarly, equitable estoppel arguments and California's policy favoring arbitration cannot overcome the clear language of these two contracts. If the Pilot AI defendants wish to bring Ms. Moore's claims against them into arbitration, they must seek to do so through their own petition to compel arbitration under their own arbitration clause, not by way of joinder to motion of the TriNet entities. Defendants' evidentiary objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Moore is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT WESTFIELD GROUP, LLC's Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint Defendant Emporium Mall, LLC's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is granted in its entirety without leave to amend but without prejudice to plaintiff Luis Galicia filing a motion to amend if and when he ever obtains sufficient information to allege punitive damages liability against Emporium Mall. Mr. Galicia concedes that the language "For costs of suit and attorney fees" should be stricken from the first amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: JOHN PHELPS VS. GEORGE THOMAS MCBRATNEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2018, Line 16, DEFENDANTs PHIL KLAASSEN, ELAINE PRICE, HEATHER PARSONS, GEORGE MCBRATNEY, and TREVOR MCBRATNEY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendants George and Trevor McBratney, Phil Klaassen, Elaine Price, and Heather Parsons' anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff John Phelps is denied as to both causes of action. Defendants fail to satisfy the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. Mr. Phelps alleges that defendants illegally recorded his telephone conversations. This conduct does not involve protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16(e)(4). Defendants' recording of business conversations regarding a Canadian dog food company also does not concern the public interest. Courts have identified a matter of public interest as statements either concerning a person or entity in the public eye, conduct that could directly affect a large number of people beyond the direct participants or a topic of widespread public interest. (See, e.g., Rivero v. American Federation of State, County, and Municipal Employees, AFL-CIO (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 913, 924.) The fact that the parties now argue over the admissibility of the recordings also does not implicate the anti-SLAPP statute. The complained of conduct occurred prior to any petitioning activity and the basis of liability is the act of recording. Mr. Phelps' request for fees is denied as the motion was not frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Phelps is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
